ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-29-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nancy Rasch for and with Gene Marchi.

The Court questions whether it is the best way to go forward with the Trustee serving in other capacities. Ms. Rasch stated that Ms. Lubin has not been declared incompetent, that the Special Needs Trust ("SNT") was set up because she received distribution from her Mother and was receiving benefits at the time, that she does not qualify for a conservatorship and that she has capacity to select who she wants. Ms. Rasch also stated that Ms. Lubin makes her own decisions and functions on her own most of the time, that she acknowledges when she needs assistance, that Mr. Marchi assisted Ms. Lubin with managing benefits, that he helped her get the PG&E pension, and that Mr. Marchi is power of attorney ("POA") on the bank account, but it is managed by Ms. Lubin. Mr. Marchi stated that they put a cap on what the Sister will get from the SNT, that he tracks monthly and that the Sister assist with grocery shopping and cleaning. The Fifth Account and Report, etc. is taken under submission. The Court stated that it is appropriate to take fees on account for acting as Trustee, but not for POA or care manager.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (210 27th Street, San Francisco, Ca)
Off Calendar Dec-10-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 291262 (The Penny Pietruszewski Trust).

Appearances: Nancy Rasch for Gene Marchi (Trustee). Ernest Der for Susan Ford (Trustee).

Ms. Rasch informed the Court that she was approaced by a potential over bidder that stated that the funds were held in exchange and that they could have them within 2 hours. There was no objection by any party. The Court ordered the funds due by noon. The property sold for $1,239,500.00 (100%). The Court confirmed the sale of 20% undivided interest in the real property. The Court granted the petitions, (subject to the written orders). The amounts of the additional bonds are to be determined.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR#5041. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.
